intro: Spyridium vexilliferum, also known as the Flaggy Spyridium, is a small shrub native to the eastern regions of Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing the risk of infection. The bioactive constituents of Spyridium vexilliferum include iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
